pre_game.message: Hey, I'm C. It's getting a bit restless in here, don't you think? I'm already planning a dream trip to Japan in my head to pass the time—do you have something big you're playing for?
Hey C, I'm A. Japan sounds incredible, but I'm focused on something a bit more grounded—my family needs a place to live, and I'm determined to get us that deposit money.

That’s a really noble goal, A—family is everything. My Japan trip is the dream, but I’m also here to look out for my loved ones. It sounds like we’re both pretty determined to walk away with something significant.
Definitely. When it's for family, you do whatever it takes. How much are you actually hoping to walk away with to make all that happen?
I’m aiming for around $15,000—that covers the $10,000 for the trip and gives me a solid amount to share with the people I care about.
That’s a solid goal, but I’m looking at $30,000 just to cover the deposit and rent for my family, so I’ve got a lot of ground to cover.
That’s a significant amount, but I completely understand why you're aiming so high for them—it’s going to be interesting to see how we all navigate the prize pool once the game starts.
It definitely will be, but I’m just hoping we can find a way for everyone to get what they need; I just know I can't go home without that deposit.
I respect that drive. It’s definitely going to be a challenge with so many people involved, but I’m hoping we can both walk away with what we’re aiming for.
I hope so too, C. It’s a lot of pressure, but we’ve just got to do our best for our families. Good luck with everything.
Thanks, A. I really appreciate the sentiment—let's hope we both manage to pull it off for our families. Good luck!
Hi there, I'm C. It’s a bit intense in here with the game coming up, isn't it? I’m hoping to fund a dream trip to Japan—do you have something special you’re playing for?
Hey, I'm C. It’s a bit of a squeeze in here, isn’t it? I’m trying to break the ice before things get serious—how are you feeling about the game?
Hey C, we actually already talked about this! I’m still feeling very determined to win that money for my family’s deposit. Good luck with your Japan trip.
My apologies, the pre-game jitters must be getting to me! I definitely remember now—good luck with that deposit for your family.

post_game.message: You took $30,000, which was over 5 times your fair share of $5,625. What was your reasoning for taking such a large portion of the remaining pot?
I admire that you took exactly the $10,000 fair share. I wish the rest of us had followed your lead, but J's large take made me nervous about what would be left.

I understand that J's actions created pressure, but taking $30,000 goes far beyond securing a fair portion. By taking so much, you effectively mirrored the behavior you were worried about from J, rather than helping maintain any balance for the rest of the group.
